title: Ninja Woodfire Smoked Salmon
description: Dry-brined salmon cold-smoked on the Ninja Woodfire at 225°F with cherry or apple pellets. The overnight brine and pellicle step are what separate this from average smoked salmon.
ingredients: 1–1.5 lbs salmon fillet, skin-on, pin bones removed
Cherry or apple wood pellets (for Ninja Woodfire)
instructions: Mix 3 tbsp kosher salt, 1 tbsp maple syrup, 1 tsp black pepper, and 1 tsp garlic powder into a dry brine. Coat the flesh side of the 1–1.5 lb salmon fillet generously.
Place brined salmon skin-side down on a wire rack set over a sheet pan. Leave uncovered in the fridge for 4–8 hours minimum, or overnight. Liquid pooling under the fish is expected and correct.
Before cooking, pat the salmon completely dry with paper towels. The surface should feel tacky — this is the pellicle, and it's what smoke clings to. Wet fish will steam instead of smoke.

Fill the Ninja Woodfire pellet box with cherry or apple wood pellets. Select the SMOKER function and set temperature to 225°F. Allow 8–10 minutes for the unit to preheat and smoke to establish before adding food.
Place the 1–1.5 lb salmon fillet skin-side down on the grill grate. Do not flip at any point during cooking.
Smoke at 225°F for 45–60 minutes. Pull when internal temperature reaches 125–130°F for a silky, medium result. 140°F+ yields a firmer, fully cooked texture.
Rest the salmon for 5 minutes loosely tented with foil. Carry-over heat will add another 3–5°F.
notes: Cherry and apple pellets are ideal — mild, slightly sweet smoke that complements salmon without overpowering it. Avoid hickory or mesquite. The pellicle step is the single most skipped and most important step — do not rush the fridge rest. Serve with capers, thinly sliced red onion, and 0% Greek yogurt as a crème fraîche stand-in. Excellent cold the next day on multigrain bread.
